titleStmt.title: SII 26.256: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 256. (A.R. No. 244 of 1909.) DĒSŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: West wall of the Karivaḍa-īśvara temple.

p.hi: No king: Ś 1450, Sarvadhāri, Chittirai 29, śu. 6, Friday, Puṇarpūśam, Kaṟkaṭaka-lagna = 1528 A.D., April 24.

summary.p: This inscription is in verse. It records that a temple to the god Śeṅgai-Vēlāyudha (i.e., Subramaṇya), with the help of the mercy of Īśurat-Timmaiyaṉ, was caused to be constructed at Dēsūr by a certain Uṇṇāmulaiyaṉ described as one who patronised the poet of Karidurga, the ruler of Kampai, Kaṉṉaṉ, Poṉṉaināḍaṉ and one who favoured a certain Pāriyāḻvan who (suddenly) appeared like a cloud.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
catāveluṅ kaiyyum §
tāṇiyuḷ śakābdam Āyiramum nāṉūṟṟaṉpattiṉ meṟcellāniṉṟatornaṇ caṟuvatāri cittiraiyilirupattoṉpatāntiyati pūrvvapakṣam
pāpyaṣaṣṭi nalcukkiṟavāramuṅkoṇṭa teḷpār puṇarpūcattu ṉāḷ paṇpāṉa kaṟkaṭakalagnantaṉṉile pārelāṅ koṇṭāṭave
tirumaruvumicurattimmaiyaṉ kiṟupaikoṇṭu cirmaḻavar kulam viḷaṅkat tecūrile ceṅkai velāyutaṟkoru tirukkoyil kaṭṭuvittāṉ
kariturka nāvalaṟkaruḷ kampaiyātipaṉ kaṉṉaṉ miku poṉṉaināṭaṉ kārpolavanta pāriyāḻvāṉukantatoru karuṇai Uṇṇāmulaiyaṉe
Digital edition of SII 26.256 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
